name: Samsung Electronics
summary: Samsung Electronics is the world's largest memory chip producer and largest holding in the KOSPI. **DAY 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday; THREE-DAY window June 7 cutoff -> early June 10, June 8/9 runs empty; deltas vs June 7): SAMSUNG +~6% IN THE JUN 9 CHIP REBOUND — AI-ROTATION DRIVER, NOT A GULF SIGNAL.** Samsung gained +~6% on June 9 in the broad Korean chip rebound (NVIDIA-Korea AI-cooperation deals + President Lee's 'KOSPI is undervalued' remarks + bargain-hunting), as KOSPI rebounded ~4.8% after an >8% June 8 plunge. This is the AI-rotation tape (post-Broadcom June 4 chip repricing), NOT a West Asia/Gulf signal. Helium inventory secured defuses the near-term cliff (carry-forward) — the +~6% is sentiment/positioning, not input-supply. PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE unchanged: Samsung (with SK Hynix) has been the SINK for the FII capital leaving India; the bidirectional consecutive-day swings show the substitution trade is two-sided and volatile, but the Jun 9 AI-deal rebound reasserts the bid (no confirmed sustained reversal toward India). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10)**: both triggers (production-halt; strike-drawdown) STAY watching — no production halt, helium cliff defused. PRIOR — **DAY 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session [May jobs + AI rout] -> 5-6 June overnight Gulf exchange): SAMSUNG -7% CONFIRMED IN THE 5-JUNE ASIA SESSION — THE REALISED VALUATION/POSITIONING UNWIND, NOT INPUT-SUPPLY.** The 5-June session realised the crack previewed 06-04: Samsung -7% (SK Hynix -9%, KOSPI -5.5% with a circuit breaker) as the crowded, leveraged North-Asia AI-memory trade unwound on the broad rates/AI risk-off (Broadcom's AI-guidance miss + a hot May jobs report 172K vs ~85K -> sell-everything-rate-sensitive). This is VALUATION/positioning (~0% crisis), NOT input-supply — the helium overhang eased (SK hynix secured/diversified inventory, carry-forward), so the -7% is crowded-trade de-risking, not a helium-cliff event. GRAPH HYGIENE: same -7% move previewed 06-04 — confirm/refine, do NOT double-count. PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE unchanged: Samsung (with SK Hynix) has been the SINK for the FII capital leaving India — its realised valuation crack is hard evidence the capital_substitution trade can reverse toward India (markets C1/E1). No production-halt announced; trigger stays watching. PRIOR — **DAY 97 (4 June): SAMSUNG -7% ON BROADCOM'S AI-GUIDANCE MISS — THE FIRST VALUATION CRACK IN THE SUPERCYCLE.** Broadcom's after-the-bell guide miss (AVGO -12.6%, SMH -4.4% live) cracked the crowded AI-memory trade: Samsung -7%, SK Hynix -9%, KOSPI -5.3%. This is VALUATION (~0% crisis), NOT helium — SK Hynix said it secured sufficient inventory (intel A11), easing the near-term Korea cliff; Samsung's position not separately confirmed today (64.7% Qatar concentration, HeRS recycling ~18.6%). PORTFOLIO SIGNIFICANCE: Samsung (with SK Hynix) has been the SINK for the FII capital leaving India — its first valuation crack is the first hard evidence the capital_substitution trade can reverse toward India (markets C1/E1). No production-halt announced; trigger stays watching. PRIOR — **DAY 96 (3 June): THE AI-MEMORY SUPERCYCLE STAYED DECOUPLED THROUGH THE FIRST CASUALTY DAY — KOSPI +0.1% / +31.5% MoM, SMH +26.1% MoM, with Samsung + SK Hynix still ~half the index.** Samsung remains the destination (with SK Hynix) of the FII capital leaving India, and the AI complex was untouched by the inflation-regime metals selloff that hit gold/silver on 3 June. The one live tail is helium: Korea fab inventories are estimated to last 'until June' (now INSIDE the buffer window), with Samsung at 64.7% Qatar concentration and HeRS recycling covering only ~18.6% of consumption — the most reversible risk to the trade if a fab-halt materialises. Linde +2.3% on a broad-metals-selloff day is the cleanest helium-scarcity tell; NO Samsung/SK Hynix production-halt has been announced (trigger stays watching). PRIOR — **SAMSUNG CROSSED $1 TRILLION MARKET CAP ON MAY 6 AFTER +15% SINGLE-DAY MOVE — SUSTAINED AI MEMORY/HBM DEMAND. SAMSUNG + SK HYNIX NOW ~42.2% OF KOSPI WEIGHTING — INDEX MOVE IS THE CHIP RALLY. THE TRIPLE-STRESSOR STACK (HELIUM, 18-DAY STRIKE, CAPACITY-ZERO) HAS BEEN SUBSTANTIALLY RELIEVED. HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL QUALIFICATION → JUNE SUPPLY RAMP POSITIONED. AI-CYCLE REGIME IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER FOR 2026. — SK HYNIX +9.5% ON HBM4/NVIDIA PARTNERSHIP. AI-CYCLE REGIME IS THE DOMINANT NON-WAR DRIVER. THE TRIPLE-STRESSOR STACK (HELIUM, 18-DAY STRIKE, CAPACITY-ZERO) HAS BEEN SUBSTANTIALLY RELIEVED.** Per markets dossier C2: Nvidia Q1 (Feb-Apr) earnings released May 21: profit +200%+ YoY, revenue +85% YoY → AI demand thesis affirmed at scale → chip-cycle bid revalidated. Samsung Electronics +7.5% May 22 after labor-union strike threat averted Wed late; SK Hynix +9.5% on HBM4/Nvidia partnership; TSMC +3% on May 22; KOSPI +~8% single-day Thursday, +22.8% 1M. **The helium-mitigation thesis structurally validated**: Samsung HBM4 cleared Nvidia + AMD final tests with June supply ramp positioned. **Reversibility low**: AI-cycle is multi-quarter regime. **Samsung's June HBM4 supply ramp on schedule = Kornbluth helium thesis substantially falsified**. **Cross-asset confirmation**: USD/KRW ₩1,520.53 (+1.1% 1D, +2.7% MoM) — won weakening DESPITE KOSPI surge = capital-direction divergence (foreign FX outflow continues even as equity inflows). PRIOR (May 22): **SAMSUNG HBM4 CLEARED NVIDIA + AMD FINAL QUALITY TESTS — FULL-SCALE SUPPLY EXPECTED TO BEGIN JUNE 2026. THE HELIUM-MITIGATION THESIS IS NOW STRUCTURALLY VALIDATED.** As of mid-May 2026, Samsung passed final quality tests for HBM4 from Nvidia AND AMD (Digitimes / Trendforce). **Critical signal**: Samsung HBM4 supply will ramp in June 2026 regardless of Qatar Ras Laffan force majeure status — the immediate-60-day production-cut path Kornbluth identified is now the LESS likely branch. **SK Hynix Q1 call disclosed 'sufficient helium inventory' + diversified suppliers (CNBC April 23)** + Korean chipmaker stockpiling diversification (Digitimes April 9 — long-term US helium contracts). **Samsung HeRS recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption** — long-term constraint persists, but immediate production-cut risk channel CLOSES. **The Kornbluth thesis is materially constrained**: helium clock Day 7 of 14 lengthens; 60-day mitigation-through-inventory-and-substitution path is now the MORE LIKELY branch. **SMH +2.1% 1D / +20.3% 1M / +40.4% 3M** on Nvidia Q1 earnings beat + helium-mitigation thesis. **Falsifiable**: Samsung or SK Hynix announces HBM4 production-cut guidance within 60 days = Kornbluth thesis confirmed; June HBM4 supply ramp executes on schedule = thesis substantially falsified. PRIOR (May 19 — Monday): **A10 KOSPI RALLYING MONDAY — SAMSUNG HBM4 ANNOUNCEMENT IS COMMITMENT TO AI ACCELERATOR LEADERSHIP; 11 'EMPEROR STOCKS' TRADING ABOVE ₩1M HITS RECORD.** Samsung announced its next-gen HBM4 memory aimed at AI accelerator leadership. SK Hynix trades at ₩1.836M (+0.93%). Brokerages (Eugene Investment, Mirae Asset) maintained ₩3.2M SK Hynix target prices and ₩10,000 KOSPI outlook. **The Monday KOSPI behavior is the cleanest demonstration this cycle that the Friday -6.1% break was Samsung-strike-and-NYT-prep priced in at the close, not a leading indicator of further drawdown** — Friday's move was the panic, not the trend. **Samsung 18-day strike still scheduled May 21 — 3 days from start.** Helium clock Day 3 of 14 (TrendForce two-week count); no new allocation notice from Samsung, SK Hynix, or TSMC through Monday morning IST. **Kornbluth thesis (Monday's take)**: HBM4 announcement is the cleanest 'Samsung intends to lead AI accelerator demand' signal of the year — and a memory leader cannot lead without helium-dependent EUV throughput. Monday's KOSPI rally on the HBM4 announcement is a *commitment* to AI capacity, which **compounds the helium constraint rather than relieving it** — Samsung needs more helium to scale HBM4, not less. The 200-specialised-container stranded-supply problem and 6N-grade bottleneck remain operative. **KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric but the Monday rally has weakened the near-term asymmetry.** **PRIOR (May 17): SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE MAY 21 — 4 DAYS FROM START, NO NEW RESOLUTION DEVELOPMENT OVER THE WEEKEND.** Korea Herald / Tom's Hardware / Trading Key carried over from May 15-16 the unresolved union-management negotiation. Samsung labor union strike action remains scheduled May 21 to June 7 (18 days) with ~45,000 workers. **KOSPI/SMH puts before May 21 remain asymmetric per yesterday's Kornbluth take. JPMorgan worst-case loss ₩43T anchor stands.** The Friday KOSPI -6.1% partial pre-validation of the trigger from yesterday's brief carries forward; the live test opens Wednesday May 20-21. **Markets closed Sunday — operative price is Friday May 15 close (Samsung Electronics -8.6% Friday).** **Monday May 18 open is the first test of whether Friday's selloff was Samsung-strike priced-in or Iran-preparation-not-yet-priced-in.** **PRIOR (May 16):** Samsung Electronics is the world's largest memory chip producer and largest holding in the KOSPI (Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI weight). **A3 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE MAY 21 CONFIRMED; LAST-DITCH NEGOTIATIONS COLLAPSED MAY 12-13 after nearly 17 hours of talks at the National Labor Relations Commission office in Sejong** (CONFIRMED — Korea Herald May 13-14, Korea Times, Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, UPI, plataformamedia, Manila Times, Star, humanresourcesonline). **NSEU (Choi Seung-ho chair)** confirmed it will proceed with the 18-day strike May 21 to June 7. Demands: removal of bonus cap (currently 50% of base salary), allocation of 15% of annual operating profit to performance bonuses, formalisation in employment contracts; Samsung offered one-time 2026 payment but refused permanent structural changes. **The union expects at least 50,000 members to join.** **JPMORGAN HAS ESTIMATED POTENTIAL LOSSES OF UP TO ₩43 TRILLION ($28.8 BILLION) FROM A PROLONGED STRIKE** — materially larger than the ~₩1 trillion/$700M per day figure that has been the public anchor (Tom's Hardware). **April one-day strike caused 18% drop in memory fab output and 58% drop in foundry output on the affected night shift.** PM Kim Min-seok called an emergency ministerial meeting. **Samsung Electronics -8.6% Friday May 15**; Samsung + SK Hynix = 42.2% of KOSPI cap-weighted. **The 'Samsung 18-day strike → KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start' trigger added yesterday is partially pre-validated by Thursday's -6.1% KOSPI; the JPMorgan ₩43T anchor is materially worse than the figures yesterday's brief assumed.** PRIOR (May 15): **A9 SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE PLANNED FROM MAY 21** after talks broke down May 12-13 (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Korea Herald, Korea Times, Manila Times, Star, UPI). Union demands removing the existing bonus cap, allocating 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses, and formalising terms in employment contracts. **April's one-day strike already caused an 18% drop in memory fab output and a 58% drop in foundry output on the affected night shift**, with full-stoppage projections at approximately 1 trillion won ($700M) per day. PM Kim Min-seok has called an emergency ministerial meeting. **C1 KOSPI -6.1% TO 7,493.20 was driven in part by Samsung Electronics intraday -5.4% from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won as the index briefly broke 8,000 first time ever then reversed violently.** Samsung +111% YTD into May 14 high. Samsung HeRS helium-recycling system operational but covers only ~18.6% of consumption. **Combined with the helium-clock-expiry window of May 16-23 and SK Hynix capacity-zero, Samsung is at the center of the Korean fab complex's three simultaneous supply-side stressors.** **NEW TRIGGER**: 'Samsung announces production halt or supply guidance cut within 60 days' — would land Phil Kornbluth's helium-tsunami thesis. Falsification: spot helium retracing to pre-war by July 1.

current.attribution: AI-ROTATION driver (post-Broadcom June 4 chip repricing), NOT a Gulf signal (~0% crisis). Helium inventory secured defuses the near-term cliff (carry-forward) — the +~6% is sentiment/positioning, not input-supply.

active_triggers.condition: Samsung announces production halt or supply guidance cut within 60 days
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS watching. No production halt or guidance cut announced; the helium near-term cliff remains defused by secured inventory. Jun 9 +~6% was an AI-rotation rebound, not an input-supply event.

active_triggers.condition: Samsung 18-day strike triggers KOSPI/SMH drawdown >5% within 5 sessions of May 21 start
active_triggers.note_2026-06-10: STAYS watching. No fresh strike-driven drawdown this window; the recent KOSPI/Samsung volatility (Jun 8 plunge, Jun 9 rebound) was AI-valuation/positioning, not strike-driven.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: Samsung +~6% (with SK Hynix +~6%) helped drive KOSPI's ~4.8% Jun 9 rebound (NVIDIA-Korea AI deals + Lee 'undervalued') after a >8% Jun 8 plunge — the concentration edge fired UP this window after firing DOWN on Jun 5. Weight held at 9.0 (established directness-1 concentration edge).

recent_signals.headline: Samsung -6.1% in the KOSPI circuit-breaker week -- the memory complex hit by IDC's record -13% 2026 smartphone-volume forecast (demand-side, non-war) layered on the post-Broadcom AI unwind and the structural helium overhang (Korean fabs rationing ultra-pure helium, ~6-mo inventories). VKOSPI at a record.

No production-halt; both triggers stay watching.

recent_signals.headline: **SAMSUNG LABOR STRIKE RISK MAY 21 — UP TO ₩31T OPERATING PROFIT AT RISK; KOREAN 'AI PROFIT REDISTRIBUTION' POLICY OVERHANG SPOOKING FOREIGN TECH BUYERS.** Samsung Electronics labor negotiations broke down per TradingKey. The Samsung HBM4 mass-production schedule pushed to February 2026 per Digitimes — Samsung leading 12-layer shipments. **The strike risk is an idiosyncratic event riding on top of the helium-clock background overhang** — separable from the Iran cascade but ADDITIVE to memory-sector pressure. The 'AI profit redistribution' policy overhang persists until Korean government clarifies — drove 8th consecutive session of foreign selling in KOSPI on Tuesday.
recent_signals.verification: Samsung labor strike May 21 risk = REPORTED (TradingKey); ₩31T operating profit at risk = REPORTED; HBM4 production schedule = REPORTED (Digitimes); foreign selling 8th consecutive session = CONFIRMED.

recent_signals.headline: **SAMSUNG 18-DAY STRIKE CONFIRMED MAY 21-JUNE 7** (CONFIRMED — Bloomberg, Tom's Hardware, Korea Herald, Korea Times, Manila Times). Largest labor union failed to reach wage deal May 12-13 after two days of marathon government-mediated talks. Union demands: remove bonus cap, allocate 15% of operating profit to worker bonuses, formalise in employment contracts. **April's one-day strike caused 18% drop in memory fab output, 58% drop in foundry output per shift; full-stoppage projections ~1 trillion won ($700M) per day.** PM Kim Min-seok called emergency ministerial meeting. Samsung Electronics intraday -5.4% May 15 from prior-day all-time high 296,000 won as KOSPI reversed from 8,000 milestone to close -6.1% at 7,493 — single largest equity move of the cycle.
sources: Korea Herald + Korea Times (Korean primary press)
TrendForce May 13 (Samsung strike contained-on-revenue-impact analyst note)
recent_signals.verification: 18-day strike planned May 21-June 7 = CONFIRMED ACTION (Bloomberg + Korean press primary multi-cluster); April 18%/58% output drop = CONFIRMED (Korea Herald primary); KOSPI close 7,493 = CONFIRMED (yfinance/markets dossier); Samsung public statements indicate continued effort to reach agreement.
